items.title: Risk-On Sentiment Prevails for 2025’s Debut, Fueled by US Stocks
items.author: James Picerno
items.description: The risk-on sentiment prevails at the start of 2025, driven by the strength of US stocks, while stocks outside the US and US bonds are faltering.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The risk appetite for US equities continues to rebound after the summer's sharp correction, based on the ratio for a broad equities ETF (SPY) vs. a low-volatility portfolio of stocks (USMV).
The bullish momentum for equities is increasingly reliant on US shares — stocks ex-US, by comparison, are faltering.

For US bonds, a risk-off trend persists, based on the ratio for medium-term Treasuries (IEF) vs. short-term maturities (SHY). Despite several attempts to recover in recent years, the downside bias has resumed.
